Fibrin D-dimer FEU [Mass/volume] in Platelet poor plasma
INR in Platelet poor plasma by Coagulation assay

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows marked hypoxemia, anemia, leukopenia, thrombocytopenia, and abnormal white cell differential (monocytosis, basophilia, low neutrophil and lymphocyte percentages). There is severe hyperbilirubinemia, elevated creatinine (renal dysfunction), high ferritin, and a markedly elevated INR (coagulopathy). These findings suggest multi-organ dysfunction, likely with hepatic and renal impairment, possible bone marrow suppression, and a high risk of bleeding. Immediate clinical evaluation is recommended.

text.value: Kristina Pocius

display.value: Neuropathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus

text.value: Oxygen saturation in Arterial blood
